coreIssue: This junior Brand Designer performance conversation is about making the quality bar concrete enough for Carlos to apply in daily work.
openingQuestion: What does strong work look like to you right now in your brand design projects, and where does that still feel hardest to judge?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent project or deliverable when describing good work
whether he can point to one prior piece of feedback and say what he changed afterward
if they describe blockers in concrete terms like briefs, review cycles, or decision-making rather than vague confidence language
avoid: do not turn the conversation into a verdict on taste without naming observable design choices or expectations
do not overload a junior designer with multiple improvement themes when one practice focus is enough to test immediately
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior Brand Designer work looks like in one or two recurring tasks and one immediate practice focus he will apply there.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick two recent pieces of Carlos's work—one stronger, one weaker—and note the specific design choices or process behaviors that made the difference.
